ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general doughnut food product class; no specific Bavarian kreme-filled variant exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers doughnuts as a category; no filled-doughnut sub-code exists. Closest defensible match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Bavarian kreme donut or filled doughnut as a food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Bavarian kreme donut weighs approximately 80 g, which is a typical single-serve portion as sold by commercial donut chains in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Doughnuts, yeast-leavened, with creme filling (SR Legacy ID 18249)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for yeast-leavened cream-filled doughnut used as the primary nutrient anchor. Values are consistent with the input record (energy 375 kcal, protein 5 g, fat 22.5 g, carbohydrate 37.5 g per 100 g) and have been cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central data for cream-filled yeast doughnuts. Minor rounding applied. Calcium, iron, cholesterol, saturated fat, and trans fat values retained from input and are consistent with USDA reference ranges for this food category.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bavarian kreme donut in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 76 is derived from the closest analogous entry: yeast-leavened doughnut (GI ~76, glucose reference) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (76 × 30.0) / 100 = 22.8, where available carbohydrate per 80 g serving = 37.5 × 0.80 = 30.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (22.5 g/100 g) with low fibre (1.25 g/100 g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; saturated fat (11.25 g/100 g) contributes to arterial stiffness
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid Bavarian kreme donuts: the combination of 22.5 g sugar/100 g (with only 1.25 g fibre), 250 mg sodium/100 g, and 11.25 g saturated fat/100 g collectively elevates blood pressure through insulin-mediated sodium retention and vascular stiffening.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high sugar (22.5 g/100 g), high GI (~76), and high glycemic load per serving (~22.8) with negligible fibre buffering
type2_diabetes.guidance: Bavarian kreme donuts are unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes: the GI of approximately 76 combined with a glycemic load of ~22.8 per 80 g serving and 22.5 g sugar/100 g will cause rapid postprandial blood glucose spikes, and the deep-fried preparation further worsens insulin resistance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (11.25 g/100 g), trans fat (0.63 g/100 g), and high refined sugar (22.5 g/100 g) with deep-fried preparation generating pro-atherogenic compounds
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Bavarian kreme donuts pose a high cardiovascular risk: saturated fat at 11.25 g/100 g and trans fat at 0.63 g/100 g raise LDL cholesterol and promote atherogenesis, while the deep-frying process generates oxidised lipids and advanced glycation end-products that accelerate endothelial dysfunction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation generates pro-inflammatory AGEs; saturated fat (11.25 g/100 g) exceeds 5 g/100 g threshold promoting TNF-alpha and IL-6 activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Bavarian kreme donuts: deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that worsen joint inflammation, and the high saturated fat (11.25 g/100 g) combined with refined sugar (22.5 g/100 g) amplifies pro-inflammatory cytokine activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A Bavarian kreme donut is a deep-fried, yeast-leavened doughnut filled with smooth, creamy vanilla-flavoured Bavarian cream (a custard-based filling made from milk, eggs, sugar, and vanilla), typically finished with a sugar glaze or a dusting of powdered sugar. The dough is soft and pillowy with a slightly chewy exterior from deep-frying, while the interior filling is rich, sweet, and velvety. Originating from German-American bakery tradition and popularised globally by chains such as Krispy Kreme and Dunkin', the Bavarian kreme donut is widely sold in Singapore coffee shops, bakeries, and food delivery platforms. Nutritionally, it is an ultra-processed, energy-dense food providing approximately 375 kcal per 100 g, with high fat (22.5 g/100 g, of which 11.25 g is saturated), high refined sugar (22.5 g/100 g), and moderate carbohydrate (37.5 g/100 g). The glycemic index is approximately 76 (high), yielding a glycemic load of ~22.8 per standard 80 g serving. It is clinically unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its high sugar, saturated fat, trans fat, and deep-fried preparation. It is halal-certified and vegetarian but contains gluten, dairy, and eggs.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Bavarian kreme donut?
Is Bavarian donut suitable for diabetics?
Can I eat Bavarian donut if I have high blood pressure?
What is inside a Bavarian donut?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for yeast-leavened cream-filled doughnuts and have been cross-validated. The original input source (GrabFood) is a forbidden primary source; values have been re-anchored to USDA FoodData Central as the approved reference. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008, not a direct measurement for this specific product. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, B-vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific commercial cream-filled doughnut product are not individually reported in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 18249 at the level of precision required; values are not available from Singapore HPB Foodo for this product category as of 2024. 2. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): USDA FoodData Central reports 0 mg vitamin C for yeast-leavened cream-filled doughnuts; this is a confirmed zero per approved source. 3. The original input data source (GrabFood) is a forbidden primary source per Project Veritas v2 source policy; all nutrient values have been re-anchored to USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as the approved reference. Input values were consistent with USDA reference ranges and have been retained with USDA as the declared source. 4. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate based on yeast-leavened doughnut data from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for Bavarian kreme donut exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. The food is sold in Singapore by commercial chains (e.g., Krispy Kreme, Dunkin') and is classified as a Western/International bakery item; multilingual names provided for Singaporean market context (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). 6. Portion size of 80 g per donut retained from input (GrabFood listing); consistent with typical commercial donut weights.
